REPO: eclipse-wildwebdeveloper/wildwebdeveloper
PR #297: Add xml extension support #266
Changed files (8): RELEASE_NOTES.md, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/build.properties, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/plugin.xml, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/pom.xml, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/schema/xmllsExtension.exsd, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java, org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLanguageServer.java
Diff (+236/-5):
PR DESCRIPTION:
A few preliminary notes:
- The API info for the extension point could probably be improved
- There 2 other commits I accidentally pushed with this change. They need to be squashed/removed.
- I had issues using the form editor to contribute an extension to the extension point (but it was working fine earlier). Please test that the plugin.xml form editor works with this extension point. 

Using the extension looks like:

```
<extension 
     point="org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml.XMLExtensions">
       <Jar
            Path="/path/to/extension.jar">
       </Jar>
</extension>
```

FULL DIFF:
diff --git a/RELEASE_NOTES.md b/RELEASE_NOTES.md
index a59bf78..1cb5de7 100644
--- a/RELEASE_NOTES.md
+++ b/RELEASE_NOTES.md
@@ -8,6 +8,23 @@ This page describes the noteworthy improvements provided by each release of Ecli
 
 📅 Release Date: mid-November, 2019
 
+#### XML language server extension support
+
+XML-LS extension jars can now be integrated into Wild Web Developer through the xmllsExtension extension point, allowing additional functionality to be provided from the language server.
+
+The extension jars must implement the **IXMLExtension interface** and must register with Java Service Provider Interface (SPI) mechanism in the **/META-INF/services/org.eclipse.lsp4xml.services.extensions.IXMLExtension** file.
+To use the extension point, create an xmllsExtension extension in a plug-in project and set the path to the location of the extension jar (which must reside within the plug-in project).
+
+Usage example:
+
+```xml
+    <extension
+          point="org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml.xmllsExtension">
+       <jar
+             path="path/to/extension.jar">
+       </jar>
+    </extension>
+```
 
 ## 0.7.0

+<!-- Schema file written by PDE -->
+<schema targetNamespace="org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">

+      </documentation>

+         <attribute name="point" type="string" use="required">

+               <documentation>

+               </documentation>
+            </annotation>
+         </attribute>
+         <attribute name="id" type="string">
+            <annotation>
+               <documentation>
+                  
+               </documentation>
+            </annotation>
+         </attribute>
+         <attribute name="name" type="string">
+            <annotation>
+               <documentation>
+                  
+               </documentation>
+               <appinfo>
+                  <meta.attribute translatable="true"/>
+               </appinfo>
+            </annotation>
+         </attribute>
+      </complexType>
+   </element>
+
+   <element name="jar">
+      <complexType>
+         <attribute name="path" type="string" use="required">
+            <annotation>
+               <documentation>
+                  
+               </documentation>
+               <appinfo>
+                  <meta.attribute kind="resource"/>
+               </appinfo>
+            </annotation>

+      </complexType>
+   </element>

+   </annotation>

+      <documentation>

+        <span style="margin-left:2em"> point=&quot;org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml.XMLExtensions&quot;&gt;<br>
+     <span style="margin-left:1em">&lt;jar<br>

diff --git a/org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java b/org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f28ca7c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+/*******************************************************************************
+ * Copyright (c) 2019 Red Hat Inc. and others.
+ *
+ * This program and the accompanying materials are made
+ * available under the terms of the Eclipse Public License 2.0
+ * which is available at https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/
+ *
+ * SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0
+ *
+ * Contributors:
+ *   Andrew Obuchowicz (Red Hat Inc.) - initial implementation
+ *******************************************************************************/
+package org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml;
+
+import java.io.IOException;
+import java.util.HashMap;
+import java.util.HashSet;
+import java.util.List;
+import java.util.Map;
+import java.util.Objects;
+import java.util.Set;
+import java.util.stream.Collectors;
+
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.FileLocator;
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.IConfigurationElement;
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.IStatus;
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.InvalidRegistryObjectException;
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.Path;
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.Platform;
+import org.eclipse.core.runtime.Status;
+
+public class XMLExtensionRegistry {
+
+	private static final String EXTENSION_POINT_ID = Activator.PLUGIN_ID + ".xmllsExtension"; //$NON-NLS-1$
+	private Map<IConfigurationElement, String> extensions = new HashMap<>();
+	private boolean outOfSync = true;
+
+	public XMLExtensionRegistry() {
+		Platform.getExtensionRegistry().addRegistryChangeListener((event -> outOfSync = true), EXTENSION_POINT_ID);
+	}
+
+	/**
+	 * Returns the XML extension jar paths relative to the root of their
+	 * contributing plug-in.
+	 *
+	 * @return List of XML extension jar paths (relative to the root of their
+	 *         contributing plug-in).
+	 */
+	public List<String> getXMLExtensionJars() {
+		if (this.outOfSync) {
+			sync();
+		}
+		// Filter for .jar files and retrieve their paths relative to their contributing
+		// plug-in
+		return this.extensions.entrySet().stream().filter(extension -> extension.getValue().endsWith(".jar"))
+				.map(extension -> {
+					try {
+						return new java.io.File(FileLocator.toFileURL(
+								FileLocator.find(Platform.getBundle(extension.getKey().getContributor().getName()),
+										new Path(extension.getValue())))
+								.getPath()).getAbsolutePath();
+					} catch (InvalidRegistryObjectException | IOException e) {
+						Activator.getDefault().getLog()
+								.log(new Status(IStatus.ERROR, Activator.PLUGIN_ID, e.getMessage(), e));
+						return null;
+					}
+				})
+				.filter(Objects::nonNull)
+				.collect(Collectors.toList());
+	}
+
+	private void sync() {
+		Set<IConfigurationElement> toRemoveExtensions = new HashSet<>(this.extensions.keySet());
+		for (IConfigurationElement extension : Platform.getExtensionRegistry()
+				.getConfigurationElementsFor(EXTENSION_POINT_ID)) {
+			toRemoveExtensions.remove(extension);
+			if (!this.extensions.containsKey(extension)) {
+				try {
+					this.extensions.put(extension, extension.getAttribute("path"));
+				} catch (Exception ex) {
+					Activator.getDefault().getLog()
+							.log(new Status(IStatus.ERROR, Activator.PLUGIN_ID, ex.getMessage(), ex));
+				}
+			}
+		}
+		for (IConfigurationElement toRemove : toRemoveExtensions) {
+			this.extensions.remove(toRemove);
+		}
+		this.outOfSync = false;
+	}
+}

@@ -47,6 +53,18 @@ public class XMLLanguageServer extends ProcessStreamConnectionProvider {
 		}
 	}
 
+	/**
+	 * Returns a list of XML extension jar paths (relative to the root of their
+	 * contributing plug-in)
+	 *
+	 * @return List of extension jar paths (relative to the root of their
+	 *         contributing plug-in)
+	 */
+	private List<String> getExtensionJarPaths() {
+		XMLExtensionRegistry extensionJarRegistry = new XMLExtensionRegistry();
+		return extensionJarRegistry.getXMLExtensionJars();
+	}
+
 	private String computeJavaPath() {
 		String javaPath = "java";
 		boolean existsInPath = Stream.of(System.getenv("PATH").split(Pattern.quote(File.pathSeparator))).map(Paths::get)
human_reference:
SUMMARY:
As mentioned in inline comment, I think in many cases, it would return an erroneous jar path.

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java:38] What about just `fileName -> fileName.endsWith(".jar")` ?
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java:43] Erroneous indentation (missing 1 tab)
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java:49] Aren't those string "path" relative to contributing bundle root?
I think this would need to be clarified in javadoc.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLanguageServer.java:70] This will in most cases be wrong: the path is relative to the contributing plugin id, not to the org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml bundle.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLanguageServer.java:73] Standard Java has `System.getProperty("path.separator")` you can directly use for that.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLanguageServer.java:79] One nice way to do it is using `String.join(extensionJarsPath, separator)`
v8_review:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds an extension point for contributing XML language server extension jars via a new `xmllsExtension` extension point. The overall design is sound, but there is a critical bug preventing any extension jars from being loaded, documentation inconsistencies with the wrong extension point ID, a listener leak, and thread safety concerns.

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java:79] **CRITICAL: Reads wrong element for `path` attribute**. `extension.getAttribute("path")` reads from the `<extension>` parent element, but the schema defines `path` on the `<jar>` *child* element. This will return `null` and no extension jars will ever be discovered. Fix: iterate `extension.getChildren("jar")` and call `getAttribute("path")` on each child.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLanguageServer.java:56-60] **Listener leak**: `getExtensionJarPaths()` instantiates a *new* `XMLExtensionRegistry()` on every call. Each constructor registers a PDE registry change listener that is never removed, causing orphaned listeners to accumulate. The registry should be a singleton (or lazy singleton) reused across calls.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java:30-33] **Thread safety**: `HashMap`, `HashSet`, and boolean `outOfSync` are unsynchronized. The registry change listener fires on a background thread while `getXMLExtensionJars()` runs on the language server thread, creating data races on `outOfSync` and concurrent modification risks. Consider `Collections.synchronizedMap()` or `ConcurrentHashMap`.
- [RELEASE_NOTES.md:18] **Wrong extension point ID in documentation**: Shows `point="org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml.XMLExtensions"` but the actual fully qualified ID is `org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml.xmllsExtension` (bundle symbolic name `org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml` + extension point id `xmllsExtension`). Users copying this will fail.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/schema/xmllsExtension.exsd:73-80] **Schema example also uses wrong ID**: Same `XMLExtensions` vs `xmllsExtension` mismatch as RELEASE_NOTES.md. This propagates the incorrect ID into the plugin.xml form editor's example pane.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/schema/xmllsExtension.exsd:32-50] **Empty documentation blocks**: `<documentation>` elements for attributes `point`, `id`, and `name` contain only whitespace. Provide meaningful descriptions (or remove the blocks) so the plugin.xml form editor shows useful tooltips.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLExtensionRegistry.java:66] **Lost context on failed JAR resolution**: When `FileLocator.find()` throws, the exception is logged but the stream silently drops the `null` result. The logged message should include the contributing plug-in ID and the path value so users can identify which extension contributed the broken reference.
